jokes.text: Just a reminder that... - Damn it. Where is Frank? I'm not missing this lion feeding.
jokes.setup: Zoo employee giving standard welcome announcement
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately interrupts with selfish concern about missing lion feeding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment - interrupting public announcement with selfish concern. Sets up his obsession with the feeding.

jokes.text: Hey, man, you know, they have those bubbles, you know, where you can pop your head up out of the ground and look around. - Yeah, you're looking around. It's like you are a meerkat.
jokes.setup: Discussion of zoo exhibits
jokes.punchline: Charlie's childlike excitement about pretending to be a meerkat
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent enthusiasm contrasts with the group's dysfunction. Sets up meerkat theme.

jokes.text: I got trampled by wild animals at the zoo when I was seven. - Sheep. At the petting zoo. - Goats!
jokes.setup: Dee claims traumatic zoo experience with wild animals
jokes.punchline: Deflated to harmless petting zoo animals, then corrected to even more harmless sheep/goats
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation comedy - builds up dramatic trauma then reveals pathetic reality. Multiple corrections make it funnier.

jokes.text: Dennis has a boner? He does? - Yes. - No, he doesn't. - Well, goddamn it, he did. I did. It's gone now.
jokes.setup: Text about Dennis being excited
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreted as sexual arousal, Dennis awkwardly confirms then denies
jokes.explanation: Dennis's honesty about his arousal is perfectly uncomfortable and in-character.

jokes.text: You... ditched me? - Oh, shut up, Dee. Shut up! God, don't make this all about you.
jokes.setup: Dee discovers she was abandoned as a child
jokes.punchline: Dennis tells her not to make child abandonment about herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - telling someone not to make their own abandonment about themselves.

jokes.text: Frank! Text... - Frank! What? Text us! Text us! Text us when Frank gets here! I can't... I can't... You know what? I can't... Let me just... I'm-a... Text 'em now. I'm gonna text y... Check your phone! I'm gonna text you!
jokes.setup: Simple request to text when Frank arrives
jokes.punchline: Escalates to screaming breakdown about texting
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation but runs a bit long. Charlie's confusion drives the absurdity.

jokes.text: Yeah, but that's, like, a sexual thing, man. That means you're thirsting for something, which means you're horny for it.
jokes.setup: Mac texts that he's thirsty
jokes.punchline: Charlie explains 'thirsty' as slang for horny
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor about slang misunderstanding.

jokes.text: He's stuck behind a woman driver and he wants to shoot himself.
jokes.setup: Decoding Frank's emoji message of dancing woman, smile, and gun
jokes.punchline: Dennis's interpretation reveals his own sexist assumptions
jokes.explanation: Reveals Dennis's character through his interpretation of ambiguous emojis.

jokes.text: Well, first of all, smarty-pants, they don't chirp. They chirrup.
jokes.setup: Charlie schooling Mac on meerkat knowledge
jokes.punchline: Mac's pedantic correction about meerkat sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfect pedantic Mac moment. The word 'chirrup' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: You keep sending updates with no new information. / No, no, I'm just keeping the whole group updated the way a meerkat might.
jokes.setup: Charlie criticizing Mac's over-texting
jokes.punchline: Mac comparing himself to a meerkat as justification
jokes.explanation: Mac's delusional self-comparison sets up the episode's meerkat theme perfectly

jokes.text: Well, did you know that the alpha pair will scent-mark the subordinate to show that it's the leader? If they find the weak one, they start pissing all over it.
jokes.setup: Mac showing off meerkat knowledge
jokes.punchline: Describes territorial pissing behavior that will literally happen later
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for later payoff when Charlie literally pisses on Mac. Works on initial viewing and improves on rewatch.

jokes.text: I also drank a case of beer, and I didn't piss myself.
jokes.setup: Charlie defending his documentary watching behavior
jokes.punchline: Reveals Charlie pissed himself watching nature documentary
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Charlie behavior. Mac's comparative competence makes it funnier.

jokes.text: A peach is a butt. You're horny for a butt. - I'm-I'm horny for a butt? - 'Cause you're gay.
jokes.setup: Mac accidentally sends peach emoji
jokes.punchline: Dennis explains emoji meaning, Charlie connects to Mac's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good emoji misunderstanding humor tied to Mac's character arc.

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late. I had... had to get a bag of bananas so I could taunt the gorilla.
jokes.setup: Frank finally arrives, explaining delay
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of gorilla taunting as reasonable excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank entrance - treats bizarre behavior as mundane necessity.

jokes.text: You know, speed's very important in comedy texting, Dee. If it looks like you thought about it for a while, it's pretty lame.
jokes.setup: Dee struggling with text timing
jokes.punchline: Dennis pontificating about comedy texting rules
jokes.explanation: Dennis treating casual texting like high art is perfectly in character. Meta commentary on comedy timing.

jokes.text: So it's like he's thirsty for a fist in the butt. I don't think anybody's gonna get that. You know? Plus, the moment is long gone.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to add to the text chain with a fist emoji
jokes.punchline: Dennis critiquing the comedy timing while Mac describes crude interpretation
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on comedy timing is good, but the sexual humor is a bit crude/cheap.

jokes.text: It was a goat, and it was a lunatic. Its eyes were going in every direction. - Oh, were they? Fascinating. Tell me more! - He pounced on me, and then all of his friends moved in for the kill. - Ooh! Terrifying. Ooh!
jokes.setup: Dee explaining her childhood goat trauma
jokes.punchline: Dennis's mocking encouragement as Dee escalates the drama
jokes.explanation: Great sibling dynamic with Dennis's sarcastic encouragement making Dee's story more ridiculous.

jokes.text: that he's already given the information and he doesn't want to have to repeat himself. - So you got to scroll up and see. - So, what... Scroll up. - Scroll up. - Dennis hates to repeat himself.
jokes.setup: Trying to decode Dennis's text behavior
jokes.punchline: Multiple people saying 'scroll up' while explaining Dennis's arrogance
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about Dennis's superiority complex affecting his texting.

jokes.text: No, it's all... - Yeah, it's all caps. - It's all caps. -Screaming? - Yeah. That's not good. - That's...
jokes.setup: Discussing text interpretation
jokes.punchline: Multiple explanations of caps lock meaning screaming
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about text etiquette. Functional but not special.

jokes.text: How about that with the eggplant? - Oh. Ooh. We're coming. - All right, let's go. - All right, let's go. - Yeah. Oh, yeah. 'We're coming.' Charlie made it work.
jokes.setup: Charlie adds eggplant emoji to 'coming' text
jokes.punchline: Accidental sexual double entendre that everyone appreciates
jokes.explanation: Charlie accidentally creating perfect innuendo. The group's appreciation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No, but nobody does it that way. The five minutes means 20 minutes, man. You can't... It's the whole reason I'm here!
jokes.setup: Zoo employee says they missed feeding by 5 minutes
jokes.punchline: Dennis explaining social convention that '5 minutes' means '20 minutes'
jokes.explanation: Great observational comedy about social time conventions. Very relatable and quotable.

jokes.text: What rhymes with 'limber'? What rhymes with it? - Hinder. - How do you not get 'timber'? - Timber.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to compose rhyming text
jokes.punchline: Charlie's terrible suggestion followed by obvious answer Mac missed
jokes.explanation: Mac's overthinking and Charlie's unhelpfulness. Timber/limber is obvious once said.

jokes.text: 'K.' - You're dead to him. - Yeah, he's blowing you off, man. - 'K' means 'okay.' - He is pissed, dude. - 'K.' - Yeah. - He is... - 'K' means 'okay.' - No. - He didn't even bother to read your thing! He just sent 'K.'
jokes.setup: Dennis responds with just 'K' to long message
jokes.punchline: Mac not understanding that 'K' is dismissive, Charlie explaining modern text rudeness
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about 'K' being passive-aggressive. Very relatable social commentary.

jokes.text: I called him the 'F' word. It's a bundle of sticks.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to explain her offensive text
jokes.punchline: Attempting to justify slur by claiming literal meaning
jokes.explanation: Dee's terrible attempt at justifying offensive language. More cringe than funny, which may be intentional.

jokes.text: Well, it works on two levels, Dee. It-it says that, uh, Frank's on fire and also that, uh, Mac is a raging flamer, which is still totally acceptable, for now.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his fire emoji text
jokes.punchline: Double meaning joke with casual homophobia and 'for now' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual offensive humor with the 'for now' showing awareness of changing times. Character-appropriate but crude.

jokes.text: I'm joking that maybe we should get the Jew lawyer and have an arbitration about what words we can and can't use to describe Mac's sexuality.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to justify her offensive behavior
jokes.punchline: Compounds offense with casual anti-Semitism while claiming to seek guidance
jokes.explanation: Dee making things worse while trying to fix them. The compounding offense is darkly funny but crude.

jokes.text: It's a gay thing, so you really wouldn't understand. Yeah, you know, it's, like, bathrooms at zoos are, like, big Grindr spots for closeted dads.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining why he's going to bathroom
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of zoo bathrooms as hookup spots for closeted fathers
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and unexpected detail delivered casually. Mac's matter-of-fact tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I sit there, eat a banana real slow, and he comes up to the glass, and he's banging on the glass like the dumb ape he is. It will bang its hands bloody trying to get the banana.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his gorilla taunting method
jokes.punchline: The escalating cruelty and his delight in animal suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Frank's sociopathic tendencies with disturbing detail

jokes.text: What about the needs of the group, Mac? - I'm gonna meet the needs of the group, Charlie, after that, but I just feel like... Are you... are you pissing on me?! - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Charlie lecturing Mac about group loyalty
jokes.punchline: Charlie literally enacting meerkat dominance behavior by urinating on Mac
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to earlier setup. Charlie literally doing what Mac explained meerkats do to subordinates. Perfect character logic and physical comedy.

jokes.text: A goat is to be sacrificed. I heard an employee in here. He was talking about it. Now, I offered to blow him, but he was like, 'Nah, I'm...' - Goat?
jokes.setup: Mac explaining how he got information about goat feeding
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of offering sexual favors for zoo information
jokes.explanation: Mac's casual mention of inappropriate behavior for zoo intel. Dennis ignoring the sexual part to focus on 'goat' is funny.

jokes.text: Are you pissing on me? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Dennis discovering Mac's condition
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact confirmation that Mac is being urinated on again
jokes.explanation: Great running gag. The casual 'Yeah' response is perfectly delivered. Economy of language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: When is the goat going to be fed to the lion? Uh, my friend engaged in a sexual exchange for information and was told there was going to be a sacrifice. - Well, he tried but I said no.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking zoo employee about goat sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Mac clarifying he was rejected for sexual favors, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe escalation. Mac making the situation worse by 'clarifying' is beautifully awkward.

jokes.text: When is the goat to be slaughtered? / When is the goat going to be fed to the lion? / My friend engaged in a sexual exchange for information and was told there was going to be a sacrifice.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking about feeding schedule
jokes.punchline: Mac casually explaining sexual bribery to confused employee
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of misunderstanding, Mac's matter-of-fact confession

jokes.text: This is the-the petting zoo. We're feeding the goats. We're not slaughtering them.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Mac expecting violent goat sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Reality that they're at harmless petting zoo feeding
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff. The contrast between expected violence and wholesome reality is well-executed.

jokes.text: No, get away from me. / No. God, please help me!
jokes.setup: Dee in the petting zoo with goats
jokes.punchline: Her childhood trauma being recreated exactly as described
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff of opening setup with Dee's trauma being recreated

jokes.text: I-I felt connected to you for the first time today. Yeah, see, that's what it's all about. You know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Group finally communicating nonverbally at meerkat exhibit
jokes.punchline: Mac feeling 'connected' after day of communication failures
jokes.explanation: Good thematic resolution. Mac finally understanding communication after day of failures.

jokes.text: I knew what you were saying by the looks on your faces. / that smile said a thousand words, you know? It was like, 'I told you so,' but also, 'I forgive you.'
jokes.setup: Gang reflecting on non-verbal communication
jokes.punchline: Their realization that they communicated better without words
jokes.explanation: Good thematic resolution about communication, slightly heavy-handed

jokes.text: Like, even though we were texting all day and we were in constant communication, I don't really think we were communicating at all.
jokes.setup: Reflection on the day's events
jokes.punchline: Dennis articulating the episode's theme about miscommunication
jokes.explanation: Good thematic statement wrapping up episode's communication theme. A bit on-the-nose but effective.

jokes.text: Oh, Frank is pissing on Mac. Very good. - Yeah. - Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Group decoding emoji message
jokes.punchline: Frank confirming he's literally urinating on Mac in real time
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of running gag. The casual confirmation while it's happening is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, he is! / He is for real. / Nice one. / Yeah. / Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Everyone realizing Frank is actually pissing on Mac
jokes.punchline: Frank's proud confirmation while actively doing it
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual confirmation while everyone watches approvingly
